source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals

confidence_rationale: Landmark in-vivo demonstration that hippocampal place-cell sequences during awake replay project from the animal's current position to a remembered goal location -- an explicit forward sweep, not just a sequential replay. The sweeps are goal-directed and terminate at the reward site, which directly supports the idea that hippocampal rollouts are initialized from current state and propagated toward a target. Confidence slightly below Wikenheiser & Redish because the seeding mechanism is not identified and the sequences here occur during awake stillness (sharp wave ripples) rather than during active deliberation theta sequences.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During awake immobility, hippocampal place-cell sequences (sharp wave ripple events) project forward from the animal's current location toward a remembered goal, traversing the intervening space in sequence. These prospective sweeps precede movement toward the goal and are not simple recapitulations of past experience.
mapping.ree_translation: In REE, E2 seeding of hippocampal rollouts means the rollout begins at z_t (current state) and propagates forward using E2 transition kernels. Pfeiffer & Foster demonstrate this exact structure in biological hippocampus: the SWR sequence starts at current position and traverses predicted future positions toward the goal. E2's kernel would provide each z_{t+k} in the chain. The goal-directedness of the sweep also maps to E3's role in selecting rollout targets based on z_goal.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Sequences are during sharp wave ripples (SWR) in awake immobility, not during theta-rhythm active traversal. The seeding signal for SWR sequences is debated -- candidate sources include CA3 autoassociative dynamics, entorhinal cortex input, and neocortical reactivation. An E2-analogue (fast cerebellar predictor) has not been identified as the SWR seed. Also: goal is a known spatial location, not an abstract harm-avoidance target, limiting transfer to REE's planning context.
mapping.source_context: Rodent (rat), open maze with multiple reward locations, in-vivo CA1 place-cell electrophysiology during awake immobility (SWR events)

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high: Nature paper, landmark result, widely replicated. Mapping fidelity good: goal-directed forward sweep from current state is structurally identical to E2-seeded rollout from z_t to z_goal. Gap: SWR mechanism vs theta-sequence mechanism may differ in their seeding circuits. Transfer risk moderate-high: SWR sequences in awake immobility vs active planning sequences; spatial goal vs abstract harm/goal.
